retiredReason: Cardinal-sin VERIFICATION FAILURE (Stage 3 FAS=18, 0V/5INC/5MIS/3UNV, 5 sins). CMA Section 14A framing, 20 March 2026 vote tally (148-32), Indonesia 43% takedown comparison all UNVERIFIED. Actual CMA amendment was passed Dec 2024, not 20 March 2026. Stage 3 output: engine/output/clause-nobody-mentioned-amendment-stage3.json. Topic can be covered as a NEW issue with a fresh primary-source brief; this ID is retired.
headline: The Clause Nobody Mentioned in the Amendment
context: Parliament passed the Communications Amendment on 20 March 2026 with 148-32 votes. Buried in Section 14A: platforms become liable for user content.

cards.big: Bipartisan support for the Communications Amendment.
cards.sub: Nobody is talking about Section 14A on page 34, which redefines who is responsible for what you post.

cards.big: The term 'reasonable steps' is never defined.
cards.sub: MCMC decides what platforms must do, without publishing any standard. Three previous actions overturned by High Court.

cards.big: Indonesia passed the same law. Takedowns rose 43%.
cards.sub: Malaysia's bill bypassed the standard 30-day consultation.

cards.big: PAS voted yes. DAP voted yes. Bersatu voted no.
cards.sub: This cuts along religious-secular lines, not coalition lines.

cards.big: Like a rental agreement where the landlord can change the locks anytime, and 'reasonable notice' means whatever they decide it means
cards.sub: Platform liability without clear definitions gives regulators blank-cheque enforcement power

cards.big: Good regulation needs clear rules, independent enforcement, and judicial review. This bill provides the third but not the first two.
